title: Bus Fare - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Bus Fare 17 Matching Service Providers ASHLAND SENIOR SERVICES DIVISION AND ASHLAND SENIOR CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral services and application assistance for Ashland senior utility and internet discounts and other public benefits. Offers RVTD bus and Valley Lift passes, caregiver resources, a library, a small food pantry, and assistance accessing food resources. Onsite volunteers provide help with advance directives and tutoring for computers, phones, and other devices. Offers exercise classes, social activities, special events, and a newsletter. 1699 Homes Avenue Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Age 50 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (541) 488-5342 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an emergency food pantry, clothing vouchers, laundry vouchers, shower vouchers, a hygiene closet, assistance with identification cards and birth certificates, and bus passes with transportation to job interviews. 535 E River Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 592-6139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JERICHO ROAD INC EMERGENCY AND HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term, emergency financial assistance. Assistance may include personal items, sleeping bags, tents, propane and vouchers, and other related items. Rent and deposit assistance may be available after completing an application and interview process. Eligibility: Qualified families, veterans, disabled individuals, seniors, and domestic violence survivors. Must reside in Redmond, Terrebonne, or Tumalo. Emergency assistance available to anyone in need in the Redmond area. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 699-2099 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and clothing closet that includes shoes. Provides financial assistance for electric bills, prescriptions, and propane gas. Also provides bus tokens. Delivery is available for people who cannot come to the food pantry due to medical or transportation issues or other extenuating circumstances. 210 E 6th Avenue Junction City, OR 97448 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday 9am-1pm, Thursday 3pm-7pm Email (541) 998-3992 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center for sheltering, hot meals, haircuts, clothing, public restrooms, bus passes, wound care, hygiene kits, OHP and SNAP form assistance, ID and birth record replacement and payment assistance, resources for housing navigation and shelter referrals, detox referrals, overdose prevention, and Narcan supplies. Community Health Workers (CHWs), Peer Wellness Specialists (PWS), and Peer Support Specialists (PSS) are available to assist with resources, connections to health care, and case management. 247 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Day Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm, Thanksgiving and Christmas 9am-noon Hot meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Haircuts: Monday 10am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, showers, laundry, temporary mailing addresses, clothing provisions, cell phone charging, TriMet tickets, shelter and housing referrals, and connections to various community partners and services. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who are homeless Hours: Center: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Meals: Breakfast 9am, sack lunches noon, hot meal 3pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that assists with food, showers, laundry facilities, clothing, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, and temporary mailing addresses. Advocates are available to help connect individuals to resources and provide emotional support. Outdoor gear provided when available. 1740 NW Glisan Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Women, transgender, non-binary, and gender-expansive adults and their minor children Hours: -Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-noon for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am -Tuesday/Thursday 8am-3:30pm for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am and 1pm-3:30pm -Wednesday 1pm-3:30pm for guests with minor children only Email (503) 248-6364 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh food boxes including meat, vegetables, dairy items, bread, baby food, and formula. Pet food is regularly available in the pantry. Limited monthly Senior Home Deliveries provided. People are welcome to receive food boxes two times per month. Also provides TriMet bus fares. The Community Garden is available to low-income families to raise their own vegetables. Water, greenhouse space, and gardening classes are provided. Gardeners give back by giving 10% of their harvest to the pantry. 17805 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Food pantry and community garden: Low-income households Formula: Age 2 and under Monthly Senior Home Deliveries: Homebound seniors age 62 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST BUSINESS MAIN OFFICE TRANSPORTATION PASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to five transit passes per month for local shopping or appointments in Coos or Curry County. 800 N Bayshore Drive Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 269-2013 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST BUSINESS SENIOR SERVICES TRANSPORTATION PASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to five transit passes per month for local shopping or appointments in Coos or Curry County. 1216 Moore Street Suite #214 Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 412-8820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift shop, household goods, bus passes for doctor appointments (no Greyhound tickets), prescription assistance, utility assistance (electric, housing gas, water, and propane), rent payment assistance, sheltering supplies (sleeping bags, tarps, and tents) and laundry assistance. Assistance is on a first-come, first-served basis and subject to available funds and amount of services previously given to an individual. Donations accepted. 132 SE H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 10am-2pm, closed major holidays Email (541) 479-0926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY OF CROOK COUNTY PRINEVILLE, OR EMERGENCY HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help with rent, utility payments, travel, and other needs. 937 NW Madras Highway Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Housing Assistance: housing cost exceeds 50% of income Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 447-7662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers financial assistance in the form of gas or grocery gift cards. Limited gift cards are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Monthly bus passes for The Wave Tillamook may be available for people without other forms of transportation. 5012 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Must be participating in 12-step recovery Hours: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 842-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED WAY MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY SAFE SLEEP UNITED BUS PASS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bus passes to partner agencies that distribute them to those in need of transportation support. Does not provide bus passes to individuals. 1910 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Varies by partner agency Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 816-3409 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASHLAND SENIOR SERVICES DIVISION AND ASHLAND SENIOR CENTER SENIOR SERVICES FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS FORT KENNEDY FORT KENNEDY ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION BASIC NEEDS JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID P:EAR P:EAR PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS SOUTH COAST BUSINESS TRANSPORTATION PASSES SOUTH COAST BUSINESS TRANSPORTATION PASSES ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY SOCIAL SERVICES ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY OF CROOK COUNTY EMERGENCY HELP TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE UNITED WAY MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY BUS PASS PROGRAM
